flashcards.back: Swing is a GUI widget toolkit for Java, part of the Java Foundation Classes (JFC). It is lightweight, platform-independent, and built on AWT.

flashcards.front: Explain the MVC architecture in Swing.
flashcards.back: MVC stands for Model-View-Controller. In Swing, Model stores data, View displays it, and Controller handles user input. This separation allows flexible UI updates.

flashcards.front: Name two top-level containers in Swing.
flashcards.back: JFrame and JDialog are top-level containers in Swing. They serve as the main windows for holding other components.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the javax.swing package?
flashcards.back: The javax.swing package contains core Swing components like JButton, JLabel, and JFrame, which are used to build GUI applications.
Swing is considered lightweight because its components are written entirely in Java and do not rely on native platform components, unlike AWT.
